msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 160 is another attachment-only continuation. I used only the three current attachments: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md; I did not use or substitute File Library material.

Turn 160 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 159:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem is exactly the over-G question: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.The attached history records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→FwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the requested condition πH∘Φ=πS.No structural computation should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+Certificates D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The uniform reason is unchanged: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The compactness coordinate remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one. For coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F, and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1--D.4 artifacts: no independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 160 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 159. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1--D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1--D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN160-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
